products.name: Dell UltraSharp U2723QE
products.verdict: The best all-around 4K monitor — IPS Black panel, USB-C hub, and factory-calibrated color accuracy.
products.description: The Dell UltraSharp U2723QE sets the standard for professional 4K monitors. Its IPS Black panel delivers a 2000:1 contrast ratio — double typical IPS monitors — for deeper blacks and richer colors. Factory calibrated to Delta E < 2, it's ready for color-critical work out of the box. The built-in USB-C hub with 90W Power Delivery, RJ45 Ethernet, and KVM switch make it a one-cable docking station for your laptop.
pros: IPS Black panel has noticeably deeper blacks
USB-C hub replaces a separate dock
cons: Stand is large and takes up desk space

specs.Ports: USB-C 90W, HDMI, DP, RJ45, USB Hub

products.name: Samsung Odyssey G7 S28BG700
products.verdict: The best 4K gaming monitor — 144Hz, HDMI 2.1, and Samsung's vibrant IPS panel.
products.description: The Samsung Odyssey G7 28" brings 4K gaming to life with a 144Hz refresh rate and HDMI 2.1 for next-gen consoles. The IPS panel delivers wide viewing angles and vibrant colors, while 1ms response time keeps motion blur at bay. G-Sync and FreeSync Premium Pro compatibility ensures tear-free gameplay. It's the sweet spot for gamers who want both 4K detail and high refresh rate without breaking the bank.
pros: Great color accuracy for an IPS gaming panel
cons: HDR400 is entry-level HDR — not impressive
Stand lacks height adjustment on base model

products.name: ASUS ProArt PA279CRV
products.verdict: The best color-accurate monitor for creatives — Calman Verified with USB-C and hardware calibration.
products.description: The ASUS ProArt PA279CRV is built for photographers, video editors, and designers who demand accurate colors. It covers 99% DCI-P3 and 99% Adobe RGB, comes Calman Verified for out-of-box accuracy, and supports hardware calibration via the ProArt Calibration app. USB-C with 96W Power Delivery and a built-in KVM switch add practical productivity features at a price that undercuts Dell and BenQ rivals.
pros: Outstanding color accuracy at this price
Hardware calibration maintains accuracy over time
Covers both DCI-P3 and Adobe RGB
cons: Slightly less contrast than Dell IPS Black

specs.Ports: USB-C 96W, HDMI x2, DP, USB Hub

products.name: LG 27GP950-B
products.verdict: The HDR gaming powerhouse — Nano IPS, HDMI 2.1, and DisplayHDR 600 for immersive visuals.
products.description: The LG 27GP950-B is designed for gamers who want the best HDR experience at 4K. Its Nano IPS panel delivers 98% DCI-P3 coverage with DisplayHDR 600 certification for genuine HDR impact. HDMI 2.1 with 4K@120Hz support makes it ideal for PS5 and Xbox Series X, while 160Hz over DisplayPort handles PC gaming. NVIDIA G-Sync Compatible and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro keep things tear-free.
pros: Nano IPS colors are vivid and accurate
cons: IPS glow visible in dark scenes

products.name: Gigabyte M28U
products.verdict: The best budget 4K gaming monitor — 144Hz, HDMI 2.1, and a built-in KVM at a competitive price.
products.description: The Gigabyte M28U packs 4K gaming essentials into an affordable package. Its 28" SS IPS panel hits 144Hz with HDMI 2.1 for console gaming and 1ms response time for competitive play. The built-in KVM switch lets you control two PCs with one keyboard and mouse. At $649, it significantly undercuts LG and Samsung while matching their core specs. It's the smart choice for value-conscious 4K gamers.
pros: KVM switch is a rare and useful feature
Solid color accuracy out of the box

products.name: Dell S2722QC
products.verdict: The best budget 4K monitor — sharp 27" display with USB-C connectivity at an unbeatable price.
products.description: The Dell S2722QC proves 4K doesn't have to be expensive. For just $309, you get a crisp 27" 4K IPS panel with USB-C connectivity and 65W Power Delivery — enough to charge most ultrabooks. AMD FreeSync support handles light gaming, and the fully adjustable stand tilts, swivels, pivots, and adjusts height. It's the best entry point into 4K for office workers and students.
cons: Colors are decent but not pro-grade

products.name: BenQ PD2706UA
products.verdict: The best 4K monitor for Mac users — factory-calibrated, USB-C hub, and macOS-friendly design.
products.description: The BenQ PD2706UA is purpose-built for creative professionals, especially Mac users. Its AQCOLOR technology delivers factory-calibrated accuracy (Delta E ≤ 3) covering 99% sRGB and 95% DCI-P3. The USB-C connection with 90W Power Delivery and daisy-chain support makes it a clean one-cable solution for MacBooks. M-Book mode optimizes color rendering to match Apple's display profiles.
features: M-Book mode for Mac color matching
pros: M-Book mode perfectly matches MacBook colors
Daisy-chain lets you connect two monitors with one cable
cons: Slightly less DCI-P3 coverage than ASUS ProArt

products.name: HP Z27k G3
products.verdict: The best business 4K monitor — enterprise-grade features, USB-C hub, and HP's reliability.
products.description: The HP Z27k G3 is built for corporate environments and power users who need a reliable 4K display with enterprise connectivity. Its USB-C hub with 100W Power Delivery, RJ45 Ethernet, and four USB-A ports replaces a docking station entirely. Factory calibrated for sRGB accuracy, VESA-mountable, and backed by HP's business warranty, it's the monitor IT departments love to deploy.
cons: Slightly lower contrast than Dell IPS Black

products.name: LG 27UL850-W
products.verdict: A well-rounded 4K monitor with HDR10, USB-C, and VESA DisplayHDR 400 at a fair price.
products.description: The LG 27UL850-W offers a balanced mix of features for users who want 4K quality without overspending. Its IPS panel covers 99% sRGB with HDR10 and VESA DisplayHDR 400 support for improved contrast in HDR content. USB-C with 60W Power Delivery handles laptop charging, while the ergonomic stand provides full adjustability. It's the reliable mid-range choice for mixed use.
pros: Good balance of features and price
cons: 60W USB-C may not charge all laptops

products.name: ViewSonic VP2786-4K
products.verdict: The most precisely calibrated 4K monitor — Pantone Validated with a built-in colorimeter.
products.description: The ViewSonic VP2786-4K takes color accuracy to the extreme with a built-in hardware colorimeter that automatically recalibrates the panel on a schedule you set. Pantone Validated and covering 100% Adobe RGB and 98% DCI-P3, it's designed for photographers and print professionals who demand absolute color fidelity. USB-C with 90W PD, Thunderbolt 4, and a KVM switch round out the professional feature set.
pros: Built-in colorimeter saves buying a separate tool
cons: Colorimeter bump on top adds bulk
